entry.title: Three Ways to Deliver End-to-End Data and AI Governance with SAP and Collibra
entry.content:
In today’s data and AI-driven world, businesses have recognized the untapped potential of their enterprise data but still struggle to take control of the data locked within their complex data environments. With many data silos, numerous applications, and constantly growing data volumes, organizations struggle to understand their data and provide users with reliable access to it, all while maintaining compliance and transparency.

This is why SAP has partnered with Collibra: SAP supplies valuable business data through SAP applications and the business data fabric, while Collibra is a recognized leader in enterprise data governance. SAP Datasphere and Collibra together support a data strategy that emphasizes governance for data and AI, unlocking an organization's enterprise data potential.

 

SAP and Collibra for Trusted Data and AI

Collibra is essential to SAP's strategy for an open data ecosystem and plays a crucial role in providing comprehensive data and AI governance for the business data fabric. A business data fabric is a data management architecture that is built over underlying data landscapes and works by connecting, managing, and governing data across different systems and applications to provide a centralized view and seamless access to data without duplication. 

SAP’s partnership with Collibra began in early 2023 for end-to-end data governance, and was recently expanded to address the AI governance needs of our customers with Collibra AI Governance. SAP aims to seamlessly integrate AI into all business processes while also assisting customers in creating their own customized AI solutions using crucial business data from SAP applications. Collibra Data Intelligence Platform is an SAP Endorsed app available in the SAP Store and offers data cataloging, governance, data quality, privacy, and AI governance capabilities.

Together, SAP with Collibra enable organizations to deliver trusted data to every data consumer and drive trustworthy AI across the data landscape.

 

Unlocking the Potential of your Data

Businesses gather increasing amounts of data every day. The sheer volume and the number of applications involved make it challenging to track the types of data collected throughout the organization, let alone manage compliance and understand the value it may offer.

There are three ways for customers to start unlocking the potential of their data with SAP and Collibra.

1. Take control of your data landscape with end-to-end data governance

The potential of data is limitless, and advancements focused on data will only continue to speed up with the rise of AI. It’s crucial for organizations to take control of their data environments now, to pave the way for adopting new technologies in the future. These technologies have the potential to transform customer experiences, streamline business processes, and create new business opportunities.

The Collibra Data Intelligence Platform is a comprehensive governance solution with cataloging, governance, data quality, privacy, and AI governance capabilities. The tools provided for data discovery, cataloging, and metadata management allow organizations to effortlessly maintain a business-friendly, searchable catalog of data and analytics assets across the enterprise.

With automated data quality capabilities, data can be continuously assessed and monitored to identify and address issues like data inaccuracies and inconsistencies. This helps to ensure that data-driven business decisions are based on reliable and high-quality data.

Collibra promotes a data-driven mindset and enhances data literacy by providing a standardized business glossary and collaborative features that facilitate communication among various data stakeholders like data stewards, business users, and IT professionals.

Finally, the platform handles data risk and compliance, ensuring businesses follow data protection regulations like GDPR, CCPA, and HIPAA. Data privacy has become increasingly important as more information is shared and stored digitally and used for transformative initiatives like Generative AI.

With Collibra, businesses can be in control of their data landscape, which is essential for any data-driven organization to succeed and will also help pave the way for AI-driven innovations.

2. Make data easier to find, understand, and trust across the organization

Unlocking your data's potential begins with understanding the data you already have and enabling your users to easily discover, understand, and trust the data to drive better business outcomes. For example, your users could combine customer data from marketing, sales, and support to create 360-degree customer views – but only if they can find and trust the data they need.

Collibra Data Catalog is part of the Collibra Data Intelligence Platform and enables organizations to manage, discover, and govern their data and analytics assets effectively. It can scan and profile data and analytics assets from 100+ sources (data warehouses, BI tools, applications, etc.) and create a centralized catalog that includes metadata with rich technical, business and operational context like descriptions, owner, source, business terms and more. It also offers capabilities such as data lineage, impact analysis, and a standardized business glossary that can increase data literacy within an organization. With Collibra Data Catalog, users can easily find and trust the data they need to make better data-driven decisions.

SAP Datasphere is the foundation for a business data fabric and provides a unified experience for data integration, data cataloging, semantic modeling, data warehousing, data federation, and data virtualization. SAP Datasphere catalog is the metadata management solution for SAP applications. It collects and organizes metadata and assets from SAP sources, and currently supports SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Datasphere BW Bridge assets. In the roadmap, SAP Datasphere catalog will expand its support to include metadata from additional SAP sources including SAP BW/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP HANA Cloud. The catalog helps you stay connected with all the SAP data and analytics assets throughout your business, providing a continuous flow of information to assist you in monitoring your business performance.

Figure 1: SAP and non-SAP metadata collected and organized in Collibra Data Intelligence Platform

SAP and Collibra are working closely together to build deep integration that allows detailed metadata and lineage from SAP sources to be shared with Collibra via SAP Datasphere, enabling an end-to-end data catalog across SAP and non-SAP sources. The initial integration will be become available in the Q3 2024 release of Collibra Data Intelligence Platform with support for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere assets. 

Going back to the earlier example, users can use Collibra to find sales, customer and product support data from SAP sources, consumer data from Google, and other useful third-party data. The same users can then use SAP Datasphere to gain access to these data products and harmonize the data in a semantically rich analytical model without duplication to form 360-degree views of their customers.

More opportunities within your data are waiting to be uncovered with the combination of SAP Datasphere and Collibra.

3. Build AI with governance best practices to maximize results and reduce risk

The rise of AI and its growing role in automating customer experiences, tasks, and decisions highlights the importance of trusted data and the need to extend governance to AI. AI can unleash the potential of your data, but it requires careful governance and monitoring. This includes supervising how the data is used in AI, ensuring ethical handling of the data, complying with privacy and regulatory rules, and addressing other relevant aspects. For instance, insufficient understanding and insight into the data powering AI can result in biased AI, resulting in costly consequences such as legal action and damage to a company's reputation. An AI governance framework can be implemented with Collibra AI Governance to assist in maximizing results while steering clear of the risks and costs often linked with AI.

 

Video: Daniel Yu, SVP of SAP Data & Analytics with Laura Sellers, Chief Product Owner of Collibra

SAP, with collaboration from Collibra, will address the data and AI governance needs of joint customers through Collibra AI Governance. Integrations with SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA Cloud, and SAP Business AI solutions will be built to ensure the development of AI that is relevant, reliable and responsible.

 

Learn More about SAP and Collibra

SAP’s partnership with Collibra will continue to expand and grow stronger as the interest and demand for data and AI governance increases.

To learn more about how SAP and Collibra work together, check out the following:

  • Register for our upcoming webinar - “Enhance your data strategy with Collibra and SAP Datasphere” July 24, 2024 10:00 AM – 11:00 AM ET
  • Learn more about the SAP and Collibra Partnership
  • Take a guided basic trial of SAP Datasphere
  • Take a tour of Collibra Data Intelligence Platform

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Content NetworkPackage Transport via BTP Transport Management service.
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog post dives into how SAP Datasphere and SAP BTP Cloud Transport Management service can work together to streamline transport of content between Tenants (Dev>Test>Production). We'll explore what this integration can do and walk you through the steps to set it up.

This functionality is available in SAP Datasphere Version 2024.14

 

 

About SAP Cloud Transport Management Service (cTMS):

Cloud Transport Management service, a service within the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), empowers users to manage software transport across various tenants. Below are key benefits of using SAP Cloud Transport Management service.

Unified Transport Management: Ability to handle transport for SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and other BTP applications, including HANA Cloud. This unified approach provides a holistic view of all transports across your SAP landscape.

Imagine an SAP Analytics Cloud story reliant on a SAP Datasphere model which access data from HANA Cloud.  Cloud transport Management service allows you to manage SAP Hana Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytic cloud.  This means if an issue arises during the HANA Cloud transport, the transport administrator can hold off SAP Datasphere transport and SAP Analytic cloud objects until the HANA Cloud issue is resolved.

Landscape Definition and Transport Routes: Cloud Transport Management service empowers users to define their SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere landscape and configure transport routes.  For instance, with a three-tenant setup (Dev, Test, Prod), individual nodes can be defined for each environment, along with designated transport routes to ensure content flows smoothly.

Automated Scheduling: Cloud Transport Management service facilitates the scheduling of imports within the destination tenant. This functionality empowers transport administrators to execute transports during designated project downtime windows, minimizing disruption.

Separation of Concerns:  By leveraging a dedicated tool like Clould Transport Management service for transport management, a clear separation of concerns is established. Developers can focus on content creation and packaging within SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytic Cloud, while transport administrators maintain exclusive access to CTMS, enabling them to manage and execute transports based on project schedules. This fosters increased efficiency and streamlines the content transport process.

Transport across different Landscape: Cloud Transport management service support cross landscape transport, For example if your have developed tenant in non-EUDP landscape and Production in EUDP landscape, DSP objects can be transport in these cross landscape scenarios.

 

Configuration

 

 

 

1) Configure BTP Cloud Transport Management Service credentials in DSP Source Tenant. (Map Source tenant and BTP cTMS)

  • Login to BTP Platform.
  • Go to Service Marketplace and enable Cloud Transport Management.
  • Go to Instance and Subscriptions
  • Create Instance.
  • Download Credentials Key. ( Download of JSON will be saved as *.txt )

 

  • Login to DSP.
  • Go to Export Transport Export> My content.
  • Go to Settings > Connect to SAP Cloud Transport Management>
  • Choose JSON text file you download in earlier steps.

Note: If Configuration is successful, you should see System Status as LIVE

2) Map Target Tenants in BTP Destination.

Create DSP OAuth Client. 

  • Login in DSP
  • Go to DSP > System > Administration > App Integration > Oaut Clients>Configure Clients
  • Add a New OAuth Client

 Create Destination in BTP Cockpit

  • Create Destination in BTP Cockpit
  • Login to SAP BTP Cockpit.
  • Go to left pane > Destination > New Destination.

Note: Create new destination for each DSP Target node. For example if you have 3 tenant mode, Dev to Test to Prod. Create separate BTP destinations for Test and Prod.

Name

Provide name of the Destination, This is to identify specific DSP Tenant.

In 3 tenant setup, users need to create 2 destination

  • One for DSP Test Tenant. (Ex DSP_TEST_TENANT)
  • One for Production Tenant.(Ex DSP_PROD_TENANT)

Type

HTTP

Description

Provide a description of destination tenant.

URL

Provide API URL which cTMS will use to trigger import during transport.

https:// <DSP Tenant>/api/v1/content/deploy/

Proxy Type

Internet
Authentication

Supported Authentication

OAuth2ClientCredentials


Client ID : Provide Client ID

Client Secret : Provide Secret.

Token Service URL Type: Dedicated.

Token service URL: <Token URL> ?grant_type=client_credentials

Token Service URL is available in DSP > System > Administration >App Integraion>  Token URL

3) Define Landscape in BTP Cloud Transport Management Service.

Configure Node and Routes on CTMS

  • Define Transport Node.
  • Go to cTMS > Landscape Wizard.
  • Choose "Three-Node-Landscape"  (or Two Node setup based on your landscape)

 

NameDefine name of the NodeEx: DSP_DEV || DSP_TEST || DSP_PROD
Description

Provide Description.Ex: DSP Development Tenant || DSP Test Tenant || DSP Production Tenant

Allow Upload to Node

TRUE.  ( For DEV Node )This would allow DSP to upload DSP Packages to this Node.
FALSE ( For Test and Production node)

Forward Mode



Auto: This would allow to forward the uploaded package to next Node automatically.

Manual: Users need to trigger forward of uploaded packages to next Node.
Content-TypeApplication Content.
// Keep it blank for source node.
Destination

Dev Node : <Empty>

TEST and Prod Node : Choose destination defined in above step.Ex DSP_TEST_TENANT (or) DSP_PROD_TENANT

 

Transport Execution

Export Content Network Package to cTMS

  • Login to DSP
  • Go to Transport - Export. - my content.
  • Choose Package and click on Upload to Cloud Transport Management Node.
  • Choose export Node.
  • Agree the Terms to use your rights to deploy the import the package in the target tenant.

Note: Same export user should be available in Target Tenant. cTMS will use export user authorization to trigger import in target tenant.

  • Click on Upload.

 

Trigger Transport

  • If the transport is scheduled, import in Target node should happen as per schedule.
  • In the case of Manual transport, Login to cTMS
  • Go to the  Transport Node > Select Target node and choose the package and trigger Import.
  • cTMS will import in Destination DSP tenant.

 

Additional Tips.

Checking log details during transport failure.

  • Log in to DSP.
  • Got to Security > Activity to check the Logs.
  • For additional information to debug.

Go to cTMS > Transport Node > Choose Target Node…

Click on "Display the log for queue entry"

 Note the Process ID.

 

 In the new instance of the browser, fire the below URL to understand more details about the reason for failure.

" https://<your DSP teant>/ contentmanager/v4/jobs/<process ID form Logs>?brief=false   "

 

References

BTP Cloud Transport Management service Help 

DSP Help

SAC and cTMS Integration Blog:

entry.title: Maximize Value with Value Analytics for SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Anyone looking out for some analytics on SAP Ariba solutions?

Starting with SAP Analytics Cloud Content Release Q2 2024 you will find a new package in your SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere semantic onboarding section by the name of Value Analytics for SAP Ariba Procurement (SAP Datasphere).

The content included serves as a broad foundation to analyze all steps across your sourcing and procurement processes done in the SAP Ariba solution suite. It comes with quite a number of data models in SAP Datasphere that will allow you to get most of your questions answered like

  • how is your company's spend behavior?
  • how can the sourcing / procurement processes be optimized?
  • how can you leverage savings potentials?

You can take this as a starter to get going. And as always you are free to enhance these data models bringing your own data and models for extension cases to get the big picture and reflect data of your company's end-2-end solution landscape.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud Stories

The package comes with three SAP Analytics Cloud stories to give you an idea of what can be built on top of the delivered SAP Datasphere data models:

1. SAP Ariba Source to Pay - Procurement Strategic Spend Overview

This story provides you with insights into the value your sourcing and procurement program delivers. It does so via providing both direct cost savings as well as corresponding KPIs. In addition, the story provides detailed data that allows you to identify root causes for process gaps, allowing you to drive continuous improvement.

The story consists of 5 pages, covering 

  • Value Analytics customer gain visibility into the ROI they've achieved using Ariba
  • Cost Savings shows spend that has been strategically sourced, price reduction from sourcing events and guided buying RFQs, savings rates as a % of sourced spend and trued-up savings.
  • Spend Compliance tracks year-over-year improvement to compliant spend put through proper buy channels
  • Working Capital measure captured and lost discounts across supply base
  • Process Improvement measure cycle times for all critical source to pay subprocesses like supplier onboarding, sourcing project, contract execution, REQ to PO and invoice processing

Savings/Benefits Analysis Summary

Strategic Spend Overview - Process Improvement

2. SAP Ariba Source to Pay - Procurement Operational Spend Overview

This story uncovers the untapped potential of your procurement practice, gaining valuable insights through a holistic operational view of your source to pay process. It will help you to maximize your end-to-end procurement efficiency, mitigate supplier risks and optimize category spend.

The story consists of 5 pages, covering 

  • Supplier Management: Onboarding of suppliers, risk exposure, geographical distribution, registration and qualification status
  • Sourcing: Awarded amounts per event/commodity and risk attached, event leading and pending savings, event participation over time and events per event owner
  • Contracts: Contract amounts per project owner/commodity and attached supplier risks, cycle times per project, distribution of contract types and contract expiration term types, overview of expiring contracts
  • Buying: spend details per cost center/commodity over time, contract vs. non-contract spend, catalog vs. non-catalog spend, approval cycle times , rejections rates and approval time per approver
  • Invoicing: Inovoice amounts per cost center/commodity/over time and cash flow related, amounts per due date

Operational Spend Overview - Supplier Management

Operational Spend Overview - Contracts


3. SAP Ariba Source to Pay - Supplier 360

This story allows you to gain a complete 360 view over your suppliers. ​
Discover a panoramic perspective of your suppliers related procurement spend, from a risk, a geography or even an individual supplier angle. This will help you to identify top-performing suppliers, mitigate supplier risks and increase your suppliers’ overall process compliance.

Supplier 360 Story

 

SAP Datasphere Models

Once imported you can find all related objects in the SAP_CONTENT space. 

The stories in SAP Analytics Cloud and their data models in SAP Datasphere combine data from various Ariba Solutions like:

Supplier Management

  • SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance
  • SAP Ariba Supplier Risk

Sourcing and Contracts

  • SAP Ariba Sourcing
  • SAP Ariba Contracts

Procurement

  • SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  • SAP Ariba Guided Buying (Tactical Sourcing)

Please check the image below to get a first understanding of what is included. 

Overview SAP Datasphere Models

The objects have roughly been separated to reflect applications in the SAP Ariba suite, using the following naming convention:

SM -> Supplier Management

SC -> Sourcing & Contracts

BI -> Buying & Invoicing 

Here is one example for analyzing your Invoice line items (based on Analytical API data). It will help you to sort your invoices according to self defined aging buckets and to identify invoices with exceptions easily. 

Data modeling has followed the layered approach as for general content development in SAP Datasphere:

In the Inbound Layer (IL) you will find the local table SAP Ariba: Invoice Line Items (LT) (SAP_ARIBA_SRC_INVOICELINEITEMS_AN) and an abstracting view on top SAP Ariba: Invoice Line Items - A (IL) (SAP_PROC_BI_IL_InvoiceLineItems) that adds realm specifica like the reporting currency.

In the Harmonization Layer (HL) on top, you will find the Fact View that combines the Invoice Line Item data with the invoice exceptions plus the Date Grid view that "attaches" the dynamic date buckets to your invoice data to let you check on due dates for your invoices.

In the Reporting Layer (RL) on top, the Analytic Model SAP Ariba: Invoice Line Items - A (RL) (SAP_PROC_BI_RL_InvoiceLineItems) defines additional calculated measures and exposes the data to your SAP Analytics Cloud story.

Invoice Line Items with due date aging information

Aging Invoices Per Supplier

In case you are operating on multiple realms, don't shy away, this has been catered for throughout the data models. So you can report on single or multiple realms.

 

Data Integration

How to get your SAP Ariba data into SAP Datasphere?

The data is sourced from SAP Ariba using the following APIs:

  • Analytical API
  • Operational Reporting API for Procurement
  • Operational Reporting API for Strategic Sourcing
  • Supplier Data API
  • Risk Exposure API
  • Master Data Retrieval API for Procurement

For replicating the data from your SAP Ariba applications the Ariba Extractor app is used. The Ariba Extractor is a BTP application  that you can deploy on your BTP account. It can be modified and extended by customers and partners as they see fit.

Please see the image below to get a better idea of the set up.

Data Integration from SAP Ariba to SAP Datasphere

Once set up, you can schedule load jobs to have the data replicated into tables of an HDI container that is attached to your SAP Datasphere tenant. The SAP Datasphere models are based on top of these tables.

To get started, you can check out this detailed discovery mission that explains the set up and replication process in detail.

 

Content Documentation

Now where can you get more info on the analytics package?

For installing the packages on SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere side and getting started please check out the documentation. This will also give you an overview of all data models included.

You can find more details on the Value Analytics for SAP Ariba Procurement content package in

SAP Analytics Cloud Content Documentation

SAP Datasphere Business Content Documentation

 

Hope you enjoyed reading this blog describing the new Value Analytics for SAP Ariba Procurement package. For feedback and enhancement ideas for the package, please reach out to us at  sap_analytics_cloud_content@sap.com

Also if you have questions and want to share with other users feel free to post a question in the community:

  • For SAP Analytics Cloud, please start here.
  • For SAP Datasphere, please start here

entry.title: Part 1 - Prerequisites and Setup Instructions for Datasphere BW/4HANA Model Transfer
entry.content:
Introduction

The SAP Datasphere BW/4HANA Model Transfer Series is intended to provide you with the guidance on how to utilize this feature to help you import existing analytic queries from you SAP BW/4HANA system into SAP Datasphere.

This article is the first in the blog post series on the BW/4HANA Model Transfer in SAP Datasphere.

Blog Post #1: Prerequisites and Setup Instructions for BW/4HANA Model Transfer

Blog Post #2: Importing Analytical Queries from SAP BW/4HANA into SAP Datasphere using SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer

SAP BW/4HANA Hybrid Integration with Datasphere

Using SAP Datasphere with your SAP BW/4HANA opens up a multitude of opportunities for your analytics and reporting landscape. Customers are simply able to add SAP Datasphere to extend their business warehouse deployment with the innovation, openness, and self-service abilities in a hybrid scenario. Over time, customers can increasingly shift the workloads to SAP Datasphere.

Customers can use the Import Entities wizard to load metadata from your SAP BW∕4HANA and SAP BW bridge connection via semantically-rich objects. The wizard creates Business Builder and Data Builder entities (along with all the objects on which they depend) in SAP Datasphere.

 

Prerequisites

  1. BW/4HANA system requirements
  2. Setting up the BW/4HANA Model Transfer connection
    • Configurations in source BW/4HANA System
    • Cloud Connector Setup
    • Live Tunnel Connection
    • DPAgent Setup
  3. Releasing Analytic Queries for Use in SAP Datasphere

 

SAP BW/4HANA system Requirements

As described in SAP Note 3383586- Metadata transfer from BW4 to SAP Datasphere, the following versions and support packs are a perquisite,

  • SAP BW/4HANA 2021 Import Support Package 09 for SAP BW/4HANA 2021 (SAPK-30007INDW4CORE) into your BW system.
  • SAP BW/4HANA 2023 Import Support Package 02 for SAP BW/4HANA 2023 (SAPK-40001INDW4CORE) into your BW system.

SAP Note 3382997 - Prerequisite Note for Hierarchy Views in BW/4 Hybrid usage

In addition, BW/4HANA 2.0 systems can also be used for performing BW/4HANA Model Transfer but limitations with respect to functionalities such as Hierarchy, CKF, RKF exist.  Hence, it is recommended to use BW/4HANA 2021 or 2023 versions as mentioned above.

  • SAP BW∕4HANA 2.0 SPS07 or higher after you have applied the additional SAP Notes as mentioned in SAP Help Document Supported Source Versions for SAP BW∕4HANA Model Transfer Connections
  • SAP BW∕4HANA 2.0 SPS01 to SPS06 with TCI Implementation SAP Note 2943200 and after you have applied the additional SAP Notes as mentioned in SAP Help Document Supported Source Versions for SAP BW∕4HANA Model Transfer Connections

 

SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer Connection

Configurations in source BW/4HANA System

SAP Note 3383634 - SAP Datasphere - SAP BW/4HANA Model Import via "Import Entities" Wizard

This note contains the steps and prerequisites to be checked on backend side to set up ESH search for the SAP BW/4 Model Import Scenario via "Import Entities" wizard. This also specifies the required authorizations required for the Communication User etc. Please make sure all the recommendations in this SAP Note are also implemented in addition to the below steps,

  1. In SAP BW∕4HANA, make sure that the following services are active in transaction code SICF:
  • BW InA - BW Information Access Services:
    • /sap/bw/ina/GetCatalog
    • /sap/bw/ina/GetResponse
    • /sap/bw/ina/GetServerInfo
    • /sap/bw/ina/ValueHelp
    • /sap/bw/ina/BatchProcessing
    • /sap/bw/ina/Logoff
  • /sap/bw4

 

 

2. In SAP BW∕4HANA, activate OData service ESH_SEARCH_SRV in Customizing (transaction SPRO) under SAP NetWeaver -> Gateway -> OData Channel -> Administration -> General Settings -> Activate and Maintain Services.

Accessing SAP BW/4HANA metadata and importing them into SAP Datasphere with an SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer connection requires two protocols (or endpoints):

  • HTTP
  • SAP HANA Smart Data Integration based on the SAP HANA adapter

For the SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer connection, http is used to securely connect to the SAP BW/4HANA system via SAP Cloud Connector.

An SAP HANA user is used to connect to the SAP HANA database of SAP BW/4HANA via Data Provisioning Agent.

SAP Datasphere will reuse the configured tunnel connection to the SAP BW/4HANA as part of the Model Transfer Integration between SAP Datasphere and SAP BW/4HANA.

 

Install and configure Cloud Connector.

To use an on-premise source for model import in SAP Datasphere, the following is required:

  • The Cloud Connector is installed in your on-premise network.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) account is available.

For the Cloud Connector configuration, you will need information about your SAP Datasphere subaccount.

The Cloud Connector serves as a link between SAP Datasphere and your on-premise systems. It is required for connections to on-premise systems that you want to use for model import. Cloud Connector is required for the live data connection of type Tunnel in order to create the model import connection. For instructions on Cloud Connector setup, refer to Configure Cloud Connector

 

  1. Configurations in the Cloud Connector:
  • In DSP System > Administration > Data Source Configuration >  SAP Cloud Platform (SAP CP) Account the SAP DSP subaccount information can be found.
  • Connect to the subaccount in the Cloud Connector.
  • Create ABAP connection “https” in the Cloud Connector.
  • Allow Resources

- InA and BW/4 Services:

   /sap/bw/ina                                                               – Path and all sub-paths

   /sap/bw4/v1/dwc/dbinfo

   /sap/bw4/v1/dwc/metadata/queryviews                          – Path and all sub-paths

   /sap/bw4/v1/dwc/metadata/treestructure                        – Path and all sub-paths

- Search Service:

   /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/DataSources           – Path and all sub-paths    *)

   /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/SearchQueries

   /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/SuggestionQueries     *)

- CSN_Exposure Service:

   /sap/opu/odata4/sap/csn_exposure_v4                              – Path and all sub-paths     *)

*) the services marked here are in addition to what is required in parallel SAP Note 3087696 for the BW/4 Model Import scenario in Business Builder

2. In the side navigation area of SAP Datasphere, click System  Administration -> Data Source Configuration -> Live Data Sources and switch on Allow live data to leave my network.

3. In the side navigation area of SAP Datasphere, click System  Administration  Data Source Configuration  On-premise data sources and add the location ID of your Cloud Connector instance.

 

Setting up Live Data Connection of Type Tunnel

 In the side navigation area of SAP Datasphere, open System  Configuration -> Data Integration -> Live Data Connections (Tunnel) and create a live data connection of type tunnel to SAP BW∕4HANA.

  • On the Connections tab, click + to add a connection.
  • Expand Connect to Live Data and select SAP BW. The New BW Live Connection dialog appears.
  • Enter a name and description for your connection. Note that the connection name cannot be changed later.
  • Set the Connection Type to Tunnel. By enabling tunneling, data from the connected source will always be transferred through the Cloud Connector.
  • Select the Location ID. Add your SAP BW/4HANA host name, HTTPS port, and client.
    Use the virtual host name and virtual port that were configured in the Cloud Connector.
  • Please make sure the communication user entered has the following authorizations mentioned in SAP Note 3383634 Solution Section Point #3. If the communication user still does not work refer to Point #5.

 

 

DPAgent Setup

Install and configure a Data Provisioning Agent and register the SAP HANA adapter with SAP Datasphere

Links

  • Install the latest Data Provisioning Agent version on a local host or update your agent to the latest version. For more information, see Install the Data Provisioning Agent.
  • In SAP Datasphere, add the external IPv4 address of the server on which your Data Provisioning Agent is running, or in case you are using a network firewall add the public proxy IP address to the IP allowlist. For more information, see Add IP address to IP Allowlist.
  • Connect the Data Provisioning Agent to SAP Datasphere. For more information, see Connect and Configure the Data Provisioning Agent.
  • In SAP Datasphere, register the SAP HANA adapter with SAP Datasphere. For more information, see Register Adapters with SAP Datasphere.

 

Creating the BW/4HANA Model Transfer Connection

After all the above setup steps have been completed, go to the Connections Tab and select the space relevant for the BW/4HANA Model Transfer Connection.

  • Select Create a Connection.
  • In the first step of the following dialog, select the connection type SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer.

 

  • Configure the connection, specific connection configurations for the BW/4HANA Model Import Connection type are
    • Live Data Connection (Tunnel): Select the prior created tunnel connection.
    • HANA SQL Access: Displays the SAP HANA host and port retrieved from BW/4HANA via the selected tunnel connection. (In exceptional cases host and port can be changed.)
    • HANA Security: Enable SSL Encryption (prerequisite: DP Agent is correctly configured for SSL Support) or specify whether to validate the certificate of the remote SAP HANA server.
    • HANA Credentials: Enter the SAP HANA database user name and password. (The user needs read privileges for the ABAP schema in the SAP HANA database.)

 

Releasing Analytic Queries for Use in SAP Datasphere

  • You have defined queries in SAP BW/4HANA and want to generate business entities in the SAP Datasphere based on these queries. You can thus use data from SAP BW/4HANA for modeling in SAP Datasphere.
  • To be able to do this, you first have to call transaction RSDWC_QUERY and release the analytic queries in for use in SAP Datasphere. An initial check is integrated in BW/4HANA which indicates supported and non-supported objects. After a successful release a query can be found in the list of released queries.

 

  • You can also expose the same query more than once, using different selections or parameter.  The system generates a query view from this and transfers it as a variant of the selected query. Assign a unique DSP-specific name for each one. In SAP Datasphere, you can select the corresponding query views using the combination of the name of the query name and the name of the variant.
  • Report RSDWC_QUERY_VIEW_REFRESH allows you to update the query view. To have the changes available in DSP you have to import again.
  • Note that conditions apply for queries that are to be released for use in SAP Datasphere. For more information, see SAP Note 2932647 defaults for example.

Known Limitations and Issues

Limitations

SAP Note 2932647 - Supported and unsupported features with SAP BW/4HANA/SAP BW Bridge Model Transfer in SAP Datasphere

Known Issues

3087696 - SAP Datasphere - SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer - Search Error while Fetching Query List

3382997 - Prerequisite Note for Hierarchy Views in BW/4 Hybrid usage

 

Summary

Now that the query ZRSA_MT_CUST3 has been released, we will see in the next blog how this query is consumed in SAP Datasphere.

entry.title: Part 2 - Import SAP BW/4HANA Queries into SAP Datasphere using SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Datasphere BW/4HANA Model Transfer Series is intended to provide you with the guidance on how to utilize this feature to help you import existing analytic queries from you SAP BW/4HANA system into SAP Datasphere.

This article is the second in the blog post series on the BW/4HANA Model Transfer in SAP Datasphere.

  1. Blog Post #1: Prerequisites and Setup Instructions for BW/4HANA Model Transfer
  2. Blog Post #2: Import SAP BW/4HANA Queries into SAP Datasphere using SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer

 

Importing SAP BW∕4HANA Models

Once the analytic queries in your BW/4HANA system has been released as described in Blog 1, you can import these analytic queries into SAP Datasphere in order to build new models on top of them or enhance them.

The import of SAP BW∕4HANA models enables you to re-use existing metadata and data from SAP BW∕4HANA systems so that you can include this metadata and data in SAP Datasphere without having to rebuild them manually. With the import, objects are generated in the Business Builder and the Data Builder. Data access to SAP BW∕4HANA is realized through these objects. The data is retrieved directly from the SAP BW∕4HANA system via remote tables.

You can then use these models as they are or enhance the data in SAP Datasphere on each level of your model - for example by adding master data to a view of type dimension.

The corresponding objects in the Data Builder and in the Business Builder are generated. The generated perspective has the same visual representation of the data as the SAP BW/4HANA query. Overview of metadata mapping and the deployment status after model transfer

Known Limitations - 2932647 - Supported and unsupported features with SAP BW/4HANA/SAP BW Bridge Model Transfer in SAP Datasphere

 

Importing Queries using the Semantic Onboarding

  1. Go to Semantic Onboarding and Click on SAP BW/4HANA.
  2. In the Import Entities Wizard, select the space and choose the connection from where you want to import the analytic queries and click Next Step
  3. In the Select Entities, search for the Query you want to import, example - 'ZRSA_MT_CUST3'
  4. In the Review Entities Section, all the Dependent Entities along with their statuses are displayed. Create Business Builder Objects is selected as None, hence only Data Builder objects are imported. But you could choose from one of the below settings,
  5. Business Entities and Consumption Models [default] - Create all possible Data Builder & Business Builder objects.​
  6. Business Entities Only - Create Data Builder objects and Business Builder business entities.​
  7. None - Create only Data Builder objects. Queries are imported as SAP Datasphere Analytic Models, which can be consumed directly in SAP Analytics Cloud.​
  9. Check the notifications section and review the objects in the repository explorer after the import is successful. 

 

Importing Queries using Business Builder

  1. Go to Business Builder.
  2. Click on BW/4HANA > Model Transfer Wizard to import a BW/4HANA model.​

3. Select an available BW/4HANA Model Transfer connection, which is already created. ​

4. Available BW/4HANA Analytical Queries to import into DSP are displayed.​

5. When selecting a query, you will see the objects which will be imported to the Business Builder and the Data Builder.​ The Import Status tells you if the objects already exist and will be overwritten or if they are new.

Note :

When re-importing a model, changes in the column definitions of source entities that generate remote tables will automatically result in updating and redeploying the remote tables. This is only possible for remote tables that directly access data live in the source. Before doing the re-import, you need to stop snapshot and real-time replication for any affected remote tables. This is necessary because of the inconsistent changes in the source.

If there is a remote table that needs to be updated but has been set up for replication (snapshot or real-time replication), you will be notified and asked to stop replication first as shown above.

6. Use the Create Business Builder Objects setting to control which objects to create:​

7. To import the analysis authorizations associated with the data in the query, click the Import Permissions button, and then click OK.​

Note: Only analytic queries that have been exposed in the source system for consumption in SAP Datasphere can be imported.  ​

BW/4HANA Model and the Generated Analytic Model in Datasphere

BW/4HANA Composite Provider

ZRSA_MT_CUST3 is the query we want to import into SAP Datasphere. Below is how the model is setup in SAP BW/4HANA.

 

BW Query Definition

The Query has the Calculated Key Figure, Restricted Key figure, Hierarchy and Text Attributes etc.

Most of the InfoObjects have dependent text and hierarchy attributes as well. For example, 0D_FC_CUST has dependent text and hierarchy attributes as below,

Text

Hierarchy

Attributes

 

Generated Analytic Model in Datasphere

Importing the above Analytic Query generated 63 objects in the data builder - including all the dimension, text, hierarchy, fact views and analytic model.

Go to the space to which the model was imported and click on the Analytic Model Tab and select the imported analytic model

 

Below is the generated analytic model based on the query definition, you can see that the Restricted Key Figure, Calculated Keyfigure, Dimensions with Text and Hierarchy associations is all generated automatically based on the query definition.

 

Example of the Restricted Key Figure

Restricted Key figure expression is imported as Restricted Measure inside the generated Analytic Model. The defined expression is imported correctly. 

 

Example of Restricted Key figures based on Hierarchy Node Selections

Hierarchy Nodes selections with-in the BW query is represented as corresponding flat selections in the analytic model. Hierarchy node filter is converted into an IN-LIST with its corresponding children.

 

Example of calculated Key figure with Exception Aggregation

Exception Aggregation with-in the key figure is retained in the analytic model with reference characteristics defined.

 

Example of Restricted Key figure with Constant Selection

Defined Constant Selection at the column level is transferred with selected dimension.

 

Example of Calculated Key Figure based on Multiple Restricted Key figure

Embedded Calculations on the Restricted Key figure is transferred. Expressions are generated with the restricted measures.

 

InfoObject conversion into a Dimension with text and hierarchy association

As an example, you can see below the lineage of the 0D_FC_CUST (Customer) in Datasphere. All the associated hierarchy and texts associations are automatically generated based on the infoObject definition.

 

Impact and Lineage Analysis

Further, Click on the Impact and Lineage tools to view all the imported objects and their lineage. All the objects part of the HCPR definition are imported into Datasphere.

 

Summary

The import of SAP BW∕4HANA models enables you to re-use existing metadata and data from SAP BW∕4HANA systems so that you can include this metadata and data in SAP Datasphere without having to rebuild them manually. Multiple artifacts are auto generated within few clicks based on the existing BW/4HANA Model definition, providing users a jump start for further unified analytics with other source systems connected to the Datasphere tenant.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, July 18th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: SAP Developer News July 18th, 2024 – SAP Developers (opensap.info)

DESCRIPTION

CAP Annual Release

  • CAP Node.js release v8 & Java v3: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/jun24 and https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/cds

Fiori Innovation Day

  • SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago: https://events.sap.com/noam/sap-fiori-innovation-day-chicago-2024/en/home

What's New with SAP Datasphere in Q2 2024

  • What's New with SAP Datasphere in Q2 2024: https://www.youtube.com/live/u5eTMpCVZ9E?si=Dn3Z3iWqNNgGRpSx
  • SAP Datasphere BW/4HANA Model Transfer Series (part 1): https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/part-1-prerequisites-and-setup-instructions-for-datasphere-bw-4hana-model/ba-p/13762642
  • SAP Datasphere BW/4HANA Model Transfer Series (part 2): https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/part-2-import-sap-bw-4hana-queries-into-sap-datasphere-using-sap-bw-4hana/ba-p/13759521
  • SAP Datasphere Content NetworkPackage Transport via BTP Transport Management service: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-datasphere-content-networkpackage-transport-via-btp-transport/ba-p/13759735

Sneak Peek into SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q3 2024

  • Sneak Peek into SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q3 2024: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sneak-peek-in-to-sap-analytics-cloud-release-for-q3-2024/ba-p/13758267
  • What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Modeling Extensions & Integration QRC3 2024 Release: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-analytics-cloud-modeling-extensions-amp-integration-qrc3/ba-p/13739005

How To Integrate SAP S/4HANA BOR Framework with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (AEM) using SAP Application Interface Framework (AIF)

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/integration-of-sap-s-4hana-with-sap-integration-suite-advanced-event-mesh/ba-p/13752546

CHAPTERS

0:00 Intro

0:10 CAP Node.js release v8 & Java v3

1:25 SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago

1:54 What's New with SAP Datasphere in Q2 2024

2:34 Sneak Peek into SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q3 2024

3:12 How To Integrate SAP S/4HANA BOR Framework with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (AEM) using SAP Application Interface Framework (AIF)

TRANSCRIPT

[Kevin] Hey, CAP enthusiasts. Well, it's here. A new CAP major release, CAP Node.js version 8 and CAP Java version 3 are out now. Congratulations to the entire CAP team. There's a ton of features and fixes across pretty much all of CAP's surface area, including the CAP tools and CLI, the CDS language and compiler, both the Node.js and Java runtimes, multi-tenancy, CAP plugins, and running CAP on Kyma/Kubernetes. There's far too much to detail in this news item, so here are just a handful of highlights.

There are no breaking changes to public APIs, and that is amazing. Of course, if you're using undocumented features, there is no guarantee that they will break. There's a new plugin API available to extend the "cds add" family. The new database service architecture for the Node.js runtime runtime is now generally available. Then the protocol adapters for Odata and REST have been completely re-implemented, and those are now generally available, too. There is now support for the consumption of SAP Cloud Identity Services in the Java runtime. And there are new plugins and updates to existing plugins, too. For all details, check out the CAP release notes today.

[Michelle] For all you front-end folks out there, there's an event coming up for you. SAP Fiori Innovation Day is happening in Chicago on August 13th. You should attend this event if you want to learn more about SAP Fiori and SAP UX strategy and innovation. This is a great event for anyone in a technical role or a role that touches app development. You can learn more and register for this all day event by clicking the link in the description below. Happy coding.

[Witalij] This week SAP DataSphere product management team hosted a session where at in this could receive the latest updates on features and functionalities delivered in Q2 2024. The session covered updates on data integration, modeling, and various other topics. If you missed it, the recording is available, please find the link in the description. There are two specific topics worth exploring in details. The SAP Datasphere BW for HANA model transfer series, which provides guidance on how to utilize this feature to import existing analytics queries from your SAP BW for HANA system into SAP Datasphere, and a blog post on how SAP Datasphere and BTP Cloud Transport Management Service collaborate to streamline content transport between dev, test, production, and tenant.

Looking ahead, the SAP Analytics Cloud product team offers a sneak peek into the Q3 release. Among the top five features discussed there are Report-to-Report Interface for Live SAP BW, Just Ask Enhancements, and Standard Unit Conversions explored in-depth in a separate What's New in Modeling extensions and Integration QRC 3 release. Please remember that the roadmap explorer provides a complete and comprehensive list of all features coming in 2024, so both SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud.

[Rich] Hey folks, hope everyone out there is doing well. I have a blog post here to share with you about how to integrate S/4 HANA BOR framework with SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Mesh or AEM using SAP Application Interface Framework or AIF. So in this blog post, the author mentions how events are becoming increasingly popular for integration scenarios, and I couldn't agree more. They take you step-by-step through the process of wiring up an SAP S/4 HANA system together with an older SAP ERP system, leveraging the capabilities of the event mesh and AIF. I would imagine this type of thing is going to be quite common over the next few years as we move custom roof to us from on a cloud. So take a few minutes and check out this blog post today.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere: How to enable the "WORKDAYS" functions
entry.content:
Dear readers and Datasphere learners,

this is my very first blog in the SAP Community, even if I've been contributing here since many years.

I myself have started learning SAP Datasphere in March 2023... but only recently I came across this topic, which will be explained in this blog.

I think, all of you know that you can use in-built functions in graphical view to create a calculated column, which can be used directly without any pre-requisites. Like string functions, numeric functions or date functions who probably the most of you have already been using.

What I found out - and is not described so far in any SAP help page or KBA is that two of them need some prerequisites:

  • ADD_WORKDYS
  • WORKDAYS_BETWEEN

Both of them need a least the factory calendar of an ERP or S4 system, stored in the SAP Datasphere space, in which they are intended to be executed.

The easiest way to fulfill this requirement is to use the Data Provisioning Agent (if you already have enabled it) to deploy the remote table TFACS, which stores all factory calendars,  from your source system:

Remote Table imported into SAP Datasphere (with identical table structure):

In most cases it should be sufficient to load a snapshot once, since in most systems I know, all dates until year 2050 are maintained. If not, then you would have to load it again, after additional years have been maintained.

Finally we can check the syntax of the function, here the example of WORKDAYS_BETWEEN:

WORKDAYS_BETWEEN Function (Datetime)
Computes the number of workdays between a specified start date and a specified end date.

Syntax
WORKDAYS_BETWEEN( <factory_calendar_id>, <start_date>, <end_date> [, <source_schema>])

Almost all parts of the syntax are obvious... except <source_schema>. The help text describes:

<source_schema>
Specifies the schema where the factory calendar table TFACS is located. This parameter can be omitted if the schema of the TFACS is the same as the current schema.

So, this means that the function works without any source schema if the table TFACS is located in the same space like the view:

You can see:

  • if both dates exist, the difference is calculated, like here 0 or 22
  • if not both dates exist, the result is NULL

To be honest, some questions are remaining here, for which I myself have not yet an answer... so please feel free to comment, and I can update the blog afterwards.

1) I tried to share my TFACS table (and also a graphical view on top of it, like this KBA proposes: 3396583) into a second space, to use it there, too. But when I trie to add, e.g. MYSPACE.TFACS, or my view: MYSPACE.V_TFACS as the last parameter, both tries result in an error message:

column store error: column store error: exception 334036: Unable to read information from table or view FHC_CONFIG - invalid schema name: V_TFACS: line 1 col 22 (at pos 21) at SQLFrontend/SQLPreprocessor/Rules/Impl/TableValidation.cpp:279; $$detail$=Statement: 'SELECT "CLIENT" FROM "V_TFACS"."FHC_CONFIG" WHERE KEY_FIELD = 'MIGRATION_COMPLETED' AND VALUE = 'X'', user: TEST_SME

--> What is the correct syntax for sharing the Table or the View into another space?

2) The above mentioned KBA links a SAP Help page which describes regarding the last parameter: Specifies the suffix appended to the standard Factory Calendar table name or the standard Factory And Holiday Calendar table names if using an alternative set of tables. For example, with suffix '_XYZ' the function accesses the alternative tables TFACS_XYZ or FHC_C_FCAL_XYZ instead of standard tables TFACS or FHC_C_FCAL. If omitted or empty, the standard tables are used.

It seems, there is a second table "FHC_C_FCAL" with which the functions could work as well. I found out this table exists from SAP S/4HANA release 2022 onwards ... but in my system the table is blank.

--> Does anybody know how the table "FHC_C_FCAL" can be filled? I looked for a SAP help page or another KBA, but I did not find anything, how to fill it.

3) The same help page describes 11 "FHC_C_*" tables in S4 HANA. 

--> Would all 11 tables be required in SAP Datasphere or is FHC_C_FCAL sufficient?

I hope you found the blog helpful anyways, and maybe this supports the one or the other development regarding the factory calender.

Best regards,

Martin

entry.title: SAP Datasphere News in July
entry.content:
Sunshine 🌞, summertime ⛱️, and vacation period 🏝️ did not stop us and deliver feature rich releases in July. There are also lots of excellent new blogs in the community and top features on YouTube for July below.

My top features in July

SAP Cloud Transport Management system (cTMS) integration

You can now integrate SAP Datasphere with SAP Cloud Transport Management service on SAP BTP to transport content packages across different landscapes. SAP Cloud TMS lets you manage software deliverables between accounts of different environments, by transporting them across various runtimes. To transport your SAP Datasphere content through SAP Cloud TMS, you first must configure your SAP Cloud TMS system. Once you’ve configured the system, you must connect it to your SAP Datasphere tenant.
More information.

 

SCIM API

This API allows you to programmatically manage users using a SCIM 2.0 compliant endpoint. You can create, read, update, and delete users and add them to roles via the SCIM API. SAP Datasphere exposes a REST API based on the System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM 2.0) specification. This API allows you to keep your SAP Datasphere system synchronized with your preferred identity management solution.
More information.

 

Elastic Compute Nodes

You can now get additional on-demand compute and processing memory called elastic compute nodes (ECN) in case certain views and SAP HANA multi-dimensional services (MDS) requests regularly require more resources than are available on your SAP Datasphere tenant. Just create elastic compute nodes, allocate the additional resources to them and schedule them to spin up to handle read peak loads. You can identify peak loads and monitor elastic compute nodes in dedicated dashboards in the System Monitor.

  • To purchase compute and processing memory for elastic compute nodes in the Tenant Configuration page. More information.
  • To create and run elastic compute nodes in the Space Management. More information.
  • To monitor elastic compute nodes in the System Monitor. More information.

In Tenant Configuration, you can also modify your elastic compute node consumption settings. More information.

 

Efficiency features

If you are following my news blogs for a while you will have noticed my preference for small features with high impact on developer efficiency in all areas of the product. There are quite a few in July.

Data Preview

You can now use the View as User tool in the Data Viewer panel to review the effects of the data access controls you apply by checking the records that another user will be allowed to see. More information.

Analytic Model

Data Export from Preview
In the data preview of the analytic model, you can now export the data as an Excel or CSV file. This enables you to share the intermediate results with colleagues, and to validate the calculations and measures. More information.

Undo/Redo
When you edit an analytic model, you can now undo or redo your changes. More information.

Diagram Orientation
You can now change the orientation of the layout of the diagram of the analytic model. More information.

Mass renaming of attributes from dimensions (Suffix/Prefix)
You can specify a custom prefix or suffix for the technical name of attributes and do mass renaming. More information.

 

 

My top blogs in July

The new integration of SAP Datasphere with the SAP BTP Cloud Transport Management service is also very detailed describe in the blog from @ashokkumar_kn  
➡️ SAP Datasphere Content Network Package Transport via BTP Transport Management service

@Katryn_Cheng  lays out the three ways to deliver end-to-end data and AI governance using Collibra and SAP tooling in her blog: 
➡️ Three Ways to Deliver End-to-End Data and AI Governance with SAP and Collibra

Check out the excellent blog series by @senthurshree and @RahulSanch on the SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer to SAP Datasphere. Dive into the details:
➡️ Part 1 - Prerequisites and Setup Instructions for Datasphere BW/4HANA Model Transfer
➡️ Part 2 - Import SAP BW/4HANA Queries into SAP Datasphere using SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer

 

More blogs to check out 👇

  • SAP Datasphere: How to enable the "WORKDAYS" functions
  • SAP Developer News, July 18th, 2024
  • Part 1 - Prerequisites and Setup Instructions for Datasphere BW/4HANA Model Transfer
  • Part 2 - Import SAP BW/4HANA Queries into SAP Datasphere using SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer
  • SAP Datasphere Content Network Package Transport via BTP Transport Management service
  • Three Ways to Deliver End-to-End Data and AI Governance with SAP and Collibra
  • SAP Datasphere – Moving Objects: Table, Dataflow from one Space to another Space.
  • Establishing the Connection From SAC to SAP Datasphere
  • How to Provision a SAP Datasphere Free-Tier Instance
  • Set up connection between SAP C4C (Sales Cloud) and SAP Datasphere
  • Integration Between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud - Blog 5
  • Unlock the Full Potential of SAP Datasphere: Your Essential Guide from SAP PRESS
  • Data Federation in Datasphere

 

Find more information and related blog posts on the topic page for SAP Datasphere. You will find further product information on our Community with various subpages about Business Content, the SAP BW Bridge as well as content for Best Practices & Troubleshooting and the FAQ for SAP Datasphere.

Find out how to unleash the power of your business data with SAP’s free learning content on SAP Datasphere. It’s designed to help you enrich your data projects, simplify the data landscape, and make the most out of your investment. Check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on  SAP Learning site.

entry.title: E-Learning: SAP Datasphere Lifecycle Management
entry.content:
